MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Hilary Huth, Esq. of Hillary H. Huth of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for Defendant Westburne Supply Co. (P.E. O'Hair).

Asbestos Order to Show Cause.

The Court stated that counsel failed to comply with the Court's May 22, 2018 preference order, and that counsel had failed to provide the confidential settlement demands as set forth in the May 22, 2018 order. The Court reads the portion of the May 22, 2018 order with regard to the confidential settlement demands in open court. The Court addressed the non-appearance by Ms. Huth at the September 17, 2018 OSC hearing.

Ms. Huth addressed her non-appearance stating that Ms. McHaffery handles the settlements and that this was the first that she was hearing that she was ordered to be here on Monday, September 17, 2018. Ms. Huth addressed the non-compliance stating that there is a motion for summary judgment (MSJ) pending, that they have not received a demand from the plaintiff, and that she could not provide an offer until the MSJ. The Court stated that it found that Ms. Huth's explanation was not adequate and that she can't just blow it off. Ms. Huth stated that she will read the Court's orders more broadly, and requested that Ms. Ly direct the email to her. The Court stated that it will instruct Ms. Ly to send future emails directly to Ms. Huth. The Court stated that Ms. Ly is an officer of the court and the counsel should treat her orders as if it were her own.

At the conclusion of Ms. Huth's comments, the Court admonished Ms. stating that any further non-compliance with this order or any Court order would result in the following consequences: sanctions and/or being reported to the State Bar by the Court.

Order to Show Cause discharged as to Defendant Westburne Supply Co. (P.E. O'Hair).

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (503/CML)

Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Love, Esq. of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for Defendant Douglass Insulation.

Asbestos Order to Show Cause.

The Court stated that counsel failed to comply with the Court's May 22, 2018 preference order, and that counsel had failed to provide the confidential settlement demands as set forth in the May 22, 2018 order. The Court reads the portion of the May 22, 2018 order with regard to the confidential settlement demands in open court. The Court addressed the non-appearance by Mr. Love at the September 17, 2018 OSC hearing.

Mr. Love addressed his non-appearance stating that the email from Ms. Ly was not specific to him and that he sent the appropriate person to the hearing. In addition, Mr. Love stated that the information from the orders weren't making it in to their system/to the "case handling attorney's",that his office held a meeting to correct the problem, that going forward they are making sure that the dates are on their calendar, and that they have been complying going forward.

At the conclusion of Mr. Love's comments, the Court admonished Mr. Love stating he was at the meeting with Judge Jackson regarding expediting the cases and that the system won't work if there isn't compliance with the settlement orders. In addition, the Court stated that it finds that there is a lack of sincerity, that it will give this process until the end of the year, and if there isn't compliance then it may reinstate the old process. The Court stated that there was a modification of the mandatory settlement conferences and the batching of cases. Inc conclusion, the Court stated that if the parties aren't going to help Commissioner Ly out then why would it continue with the new system.

The Court stated that any further non-compliance with this order or any Court order would result in the following consequences: sanctions and/or being reported to the State Bar by the Court.

Order to Show Cause discharged as to Defendant Douglass Insulation.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (503/CML)

Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on shortened notice of 60 days, unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 4, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 17, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than June 22, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than June 8, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than June 8, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on July 18, 2018 at 2:00p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers.
